title: Ruth 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Ruth
paragraphs: 1 When the judges ruled Israel, there was a time of no food in the land. A man from Bethlehem in Judah went to live in Moab. He took his wife and two sons with him.
2 The man's name was Elimelech. His wife's name was Naomi. His two sons were Mahlon and Chilion. They were from the family of Ephrath in Bethlehem in Judah. They came to Moab and stayed there.
3 Then Elimelech, Naomi's husband, died. Naomi was left with her two sons.
4 Her sons married Moabite women. One was named Orpah, and the other was named Ruth. They lived in Moab for about ten years.
5 Then Mahlon and Chilion also died. Naomi was left without her two sons and without her husband.
6 In Moab, Naomi heard that the LORD had given His people food again. So she and her sons' wives got ready to leave Moab.
7 With her two sons' wives, she left the place where she had lived. They set out on the road back to Judah.
8 Then Naomi said to her two sons' wives, "Go back, each of you, to your mother's home. May the LORD show you faithful love, as you have shown to your dead husbands and to me.
9 May the LORD help each of you find a safe home with a new husband." Then she kissed them, and they cried out loud.
10 They said to her, "No, we will go back with you to your people."
11 But Naomi replied, "Go back home, my daughters. Why would you come with me? Do I still have sons in my body to be your husbands?
12 Go back home, my daughters. Go on, for I am too old to have another husband. Even if I had hope to marry tonight and have sons,
13 would you wait for them to grow up? Would you stay without husbands? No, my daughters. It hurts me very much for your sake. The LORD's hand has turned against me."
14 Again they cried out loud. Then Orpah kissed her mother-in-law goodbye. But Ruth held on to her tightly.
15 Naomi said, "Look, your sister-in-law has gone back to her people and her gods. Go back with her."
16 But Ruth replied: "Do not ask me to leave you or to turn away from you. **Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay. Your people will be my people and your God my God.**
17 **Where you die I will die, and there I will be buried. May the LORD deal with me, be it ever so severely, if anything but death separates you and me.**"
18 When Naomi saw that Ruth was firm in her plan to go with her, she stopped trying to change her mind.
19 So Naomi and Ruth traveled until they came to Bethlehem. When they came to Bethlehem, the whole town was stirred up. The women asked, "Can this be Naomi?"
20 "Do not call me Naomi," she replied. "Call me Mara, because the Almighty has made my life very bitter.
21 I went away full, but the LORD has brought me back empty. Why call me Naomi? The LORD has spoken against me. The Almighty has made me suffer."
22 So Naomi returned from Moab with Ruth the Moabite woman, her son's wife. They arrived in Bethlehem when people started to harvest barley.

title: Ruth 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Ruth
paragraphs: 1 Naomi had a relative from her husband's family. His name was Boaz. He was an important and good man from the family of Elimelech.
2 Ruth the woman from Moab said to Naomi, "Please let me go to the fields. I want to pick up leftover grain after someone who is kind to me." Naomi said, "Go, my daughter."
3 So Ruth went out to the field and picked up grain after the workers. She happened to come to the field of Boaz, who was from the family of Elimelech.
4 Just then Boaz came from Bethlehem. He said to the workers, "The LORD be with you." They answered, "The LORD bless you."
5 Boaz asked the worker in charge of his harvesters, "Whose young woman is this?"
6 The worker answered, "She is the woman from Moab who came back with Naomi from the land of Moab.
7 She asked, 'Please let me pick up grain among the piles after the workers.' So she came out and has worked from morning until now. She only rested a short time in the shelter."
8 Then Boaz said to Ruth, "Listen, my daughter. Do not go to another field to pick up grain. Do not leave this place. Stay close to my servant girls.
9 Watch the field where they are working, and follow these girls. I have told the young men not to touch you. When you are thirsty, drink from the jars the young men have filled."
10 Ruth bowed down low to the ground. She said to him, "Why are you so kind to me? Why do you notice me? I am a foreigner."
11 Boaz answered, "I have heard all you have done for your mother-in-law since your husband died. You left your father and mother and your home country. You came to a people you did not know before.
12 May the LORD pay you back for your work. May the LORD, the God of Israel, give you a rich reward. He has kept you safe like a bird with its wings."
13 Ruth said, "My lord, may I keep finding kindness from you. You have comforted me and spoken kindly to me, your servant. I am not even like one of your servant girls."

14 At mealtime Boaz said to her, "Come over here. Have some bread and dip it in the sour sauce." So she sat down beside the workers. He gave her grain cooked over fire. She ate and was full, and she had some left over.
15 When Ruth got up to pick up more grain, Boaz told his young men, "Even if she gathers among the piles, do not insult her.
16 Pull out some stalks from the bundles for her. Leave them for her to pick up. Do not scold her."
17 So Ruth picked up grain in the field until evening. She beat out the grain she had gathered. It was about a large basket of barley.
18 She picked up the grain and went into the town. Her mother-in-law saw what she had gathered. Ruth also gave Naomi the food she had saved from her meal.

19 Her mother-in-law asked her, "Where did you pick up grain today? Where did you work? May the man who noticed you be blessed." So Ruth told her mother-in-law where she had worked. She said, "The name of the man I worked with today is Boaz."

20 Naomi said to her daughter-in-law, "May the LORD bless him. The LORD still shows His kindness to the living and the dead." Naomi added, "This man is a close relative. He is one of our kinsman-redeemers."
21 Ruth the woman from Moab said, "He also told me, 'Stay with my young men until they finish gathering all my harvest.'"
22 Naomi said to her daughter-in-law Ruth, "My daughter, it is good for you to work with his young women. In another field someone might harm you."
23 So Ruth stayed close to the servant girls of Boaz. She picked up grain until the barley and wheat harvest time ended. And she lived with her mother-in-law.

title: Ruth 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Ruth
paragraphs: 1 One day Naomi said to Ruth, "My daughter, I should find a safe home for you.
2 Boaz is our relative. You worked with his servant girls. Tonight he sorts barley at the threshing floor.
3 So wash yourself, put on sweet-smelling oil, and wear your best clothes. Go down to the threshing floor. But do not let him see you until he finishes eating and drinking.
4 Watch where he lies down. Then go in, uncover his feet, and lie down. He will tell you what to do."
5 Ruth answered, "I will do everything you say."
6 So she went down to the threshing floor. She did everything her mother-in-law told her.
7 Boaz finished eating and drinking, and he was happy. He went to lie down at the end of the pile of grain. Then Ruth came in quietly. She uncovered his feet and lay down.
8 At midnight, Boaz woke up suddenly. He turned over and saw a woman at his feet!
9 "Who are you?" he asked. "I am your servant Ruth," she replied. "Cover me with the edge of your coat, for you are a family redeemer."
10 Then Boaz said, "May the LORD bless you, my daughter. You have shown great kindness now, even more than before. You did not run after younger men, rich or poor.
11 Do not be afraid, my daughter. I will do whatever you ask. All my people know that you are a good and honest woman.
12 Yes, it is true that I am a family redeemer. But another man is a closer relative than I am.
13 Stay here tonight. In the morning, if he wants to redeem you, that is good. Let him redeem you. But if he does not, I promise before the LORD that I will. Now lie here until morning."
14 So she lay at his feet until morning. But she got up before anyone could see her. Then Boaz said, "No one must know that a woman came to the threshing floor."
15 He told her, "Bring your shawl and hold it out." She did so. He poured six measures of barley into her shawl. Then he went into the city.
16 When Ruth returned, Naomi asked her, "How did it go, my daughter?" Then Ruth told her everything Boaz had done.
17 She said, "He gave me these six measures of barley. He said, 'Do not go back to your mother-in-law with empty hands.'"
18 Then Naomi said, "Wait, my daughter. See how this turns out. Boaz will not rest until he settles this today."

title: Ruth 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Ruth
paragraphs: 1 Meanwhile, Boaz went to the city gate and sat down there. Soon the kinsman-redeemer he had spoken about came along. Boaz said, "Come over here, my friend, and sit down." So he came and sat down.
2 Then Boaz took ten of the leaders of the city. He said, "Sit here," and they sat down.
3 Boaz said to the kinsman-redeemer, "Naomi has returned from the land of Moab. She is selling the piece of land that belonged to our brother Elimelech.

4 I thought I should tell you. You may buy it back in front of these people and the leaders. If you want to redeem it, do so. But if you will not, tell me, so I will know. There is no one but you to redeem it, and I am next after you." The man replied, "I will redeem it."
5 Then Boaz said, "On the day you buy the land from Naomi, you must also take Ruth the woman from Moab. She is the widow of the dead man. You must keep his name alive on his land."
6 The kinsman-redeemer replied, "Then I cannot redeem it. I would put my own land in danger. Take my right to buy it back, because I cannot redeem it."

7 Now in earlier times in Israel, this was the custom for buying back or exchanging land. To make any deal official, a man would take off his sandal and give it to the other person. This was how they made it official in Israel.
8 So the kinsman-redeemer took off his sandal and said to Boaz, "Buy it for yourself."
9 Then Boaz said to the leaders and all the people, "You are witnesses today. I am buying from Naomi everything that belonged to Elimelech, Chilion, and Mahlon.

10 I have also taken Ruth the woman from Moab, Mahlon's widow, to be my wife. I will keep the dead man's name alive on his land. His name will not disappear from his family or from the gate of his town. You are witnesses today."

11 The leaders and all the people at the gate said, "We are witnesses. May the LORD make the woman entering your home like Rachel and Leah. Those two built the family of Israel. May you do well in Ephrathah and be well-known in Bethlehem.
12 May your family become like the family of Perez, whom Tamar bore to Judah. May the LORD give you children through this young woman."
13 So Boaz took Ruth, and she became his wife. He came to her as her husband, and the LORD let her have a child. She gave birth to a son.
14 Then the women said to Naomi, "Blessed be the LORD. He has not left you today without a kinsman-redeemer. May his name be well-known in Israel.
15 He will make your life new and take care of you when you are old. Your daughter-in-law, who loves you, gave birth to him. She is better to you than seven sons."
16 Naomi took the child and held him on her lap. She took care of him.
17 The women nearby said, "A son has been born to Naomi." They named him Obed. He became the father of Jesse, the father of David.
18 Now this is the family line of Perez: Perez was the father of Hezron,
19 Hezron was the father of Ram, Ram was the father of Amminadab,
20 Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, Nahshon was the father of Salmon,
21 Salmon was the father of Boaz, **Boaz the father of Obed,**
22 **Obed the father of Jesse, and Jesse the father of David.**
